name: Giribaile Mines — round trip with views of the Giribaile Castle
name_original: Yacimientos de Giribaile — ida y vuelta con vistas del Castillo de Giribaile
region_intro: Jogging around Vilches offers routes through a landscape characterized by natural sites, historical landmarks, and water bodies. The region features areas like the Cascada de la Cimbarra, known for its waterfall, and the Giribaile Reservoir. Trails often incorporate views of historical structures such as the Castle and archaeological site of Giribaile, alongside natural terrain. Elevation changes are present, with some routes featuring significant climbs, providing varied experiences for runners. Best jogging routes around Vilches The most popular jogging route is Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo - circular trailrunning through the natural site cascada del Cimbarra, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail that takes about 32 minutes to complete. This easy route explores a natural site featuring a waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Castle and archaeological site of Giribaile, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This route offers views of a historical castle and archaeological site. Local runners also love the Giribaile Mines — round trip with views of the Giribaile Castle, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through historical mining areas with views of the Giribaile Castle, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Jogging around Vilches is defined by natural sites, historical landmarks, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation gain. The routes in Vilches are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 577 runners have used komoot to explore Vilches's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Vilches?
A: There are over 50 dedicated running routes around Vilches, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails to explore.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Vilches?
A: Yes, Vilches offers several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989169232/cascada-de-la-cimbarra-and-charco-del-negrillo-circular-trailrunning-through-the-natural-site-cascada-del-cimbarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo - circular trailrunning&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.3 km long and features a beautiful waterfall.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Vilches area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Vilches are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364603929/giribaile-reservoir-circular-route-via-the-ariza-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Reservoir Circular Route via the Ariza Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views around the reservoir.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails near Vilches?
A: Jogging routes around Vilches offer a diverse landscape, including natural sites like the Cascada de la Cimbarra waterfall, historical landmarks such as the Castle and archaeological site of Giribaile, and views of the Giribaile Reservoir. You'll encounter varied terrain with some elevation changes, providing a dynamic running experience.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while running in Vilches?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can run past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989779016/castle-and-archaeological-site-of-giribaile" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and archaeological site of Giribaile&lt;/a&gt;, or explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364550889/giribaile-mines-round-trip-with-views-of-the-giribaile-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Mines — round trip&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through historical mining areas with views of the castle.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Vilches?
A: The running routes in Vilches are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the mix of natural beauty and historical sights, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or water bodies?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989169232/cascada-de-la-cimbarra-and-charco-del-negrillo-circular-trailrunning-through-the-natural-site-cascada-del-cimbarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo circular trailrunning&lt;/a&gt; route is known for its beautiful waterfall. Additionally, routes around the Giribaile Reservoir, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364603929/giribaile-reservoir-circular-route-via-the-ariza-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Reservoir Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, offer scenic views of water bodies.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore near the jogging routes in Vilches?
A: Beyond the trails, you can visit historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Empedraillo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos&lt;/a&gt;. For natural viewpoints, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos&lt;/a&gt;, which offer stunning panoramas.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners in Vilches?
A: Experienced runners will find several challenging options with significant elevation gain. An example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Valdeazores – Cerro de los Órganos loop from Río Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 15 km with substantial climbs.

Q: How long do the running routes in Vilches typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies greatly depending on the route and your pace. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989169232/cascada-de-la-cimbarra-and-charco-del-negrillo-circular-trailrunning-through-the-natural-site-cascada-del-cimbarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Cimbarra circular trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 30-40 minutes, while longer, more challenging trails might take several hours.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Vilches?
A: While specific seasonal information isn't available, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities like running in this region. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, but often still runnable, conditions.

Q: Are there any facilities or recreation areas near the running trails?
A: Yes, you can find facilities such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2638820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Aliseda Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt;, which might be a convenient spot before or after your run.
tips: Vertical escarpment about 20 meters high in which you can see caves carved in the stone, windows, a sanctuary, a monastery or a hermitage, most likely, rooms where monks or hermits lived in the Visigothic or perhaps Mozarabic times. when the Muslims already dominated these lands but tolerated the existence of Christian communities.

Military construction built in the 12th century, during the Islamic period. It is located 500m above sea level, in a strategic location to visually control the Guadalimar river plain, the old Heracleian route and the surroundings of Cástulo.
